title: First Unreal Project Setup
instructions: Create your first Unreal Engine project using the class naming convention and the tutorial setup choices.
requiredSteps: Open Unreal Engine using the approved class workflow.
Create a new Games > Blank project.
Leave the project set to Blueprint.
Enable Starter Content.
Name the project using the class naming convention.
Create the project and confirm it opens successfully.
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of project browser or open Unreal project
Screenshot or written confirmation showing correct project name

rubric.description: Complete, polished, on time, and clearly meets or extends the stated requirements.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but incomplete, unclear, or not functional.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Create a short note showing where your project folder is saved and how you would reopen the .uproject file.
reflectionPrompt: What project setup choice do you most need to remember next time?

title: Unreal Interface Map
instructions: Identify and label the major Unreal editor panels so you can explain what each one is used for.
requiredSteps: Open the Unreal project from Lesson 01.
Capture a clear screenshot of the editor interface.
Label the Viewport, Toolbar, Outliner, Details panel, and Content Drawer or Content Browser.
Add one short purpose note for each required interface area.
Submit the annotated screenshot or teacher-provided form response.
evidenceRequired: Annotated screenshot or form response identifying viewport, toolbar, outliner, details panel, and content drawer

rubric.description: Complete, polished, on time, and clearly meets or extends the stated requirements.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but incomplete, unclear, or not functional.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Add two extra interface areas or shortcuts, such as World Settings, F10, Ctrl+Space, or Default Editor Layout.
reflectionPrompt: Which editor panel do you think will help you stay most organized, and why?

title: Viewport Navigation Scavenger Hunt
instructions: Complete a viewport navigation scavenger hunt using camera movement, focus, view modes, and game view.
requiredSteps: Open the Unreal project and use right mouse plus WASD to navigate.
Use Q and E to move vertically at least once.
Use F to focus on a selected object.
Capture screenshots from at least three different views or camera positions.
Capture one screenshot showing Game View or a different view mode such as Unlit or Wireframe.
evidenceRequired: Screenshots from at least three different views or camera positions
One screenshot showing game view or a different view mode

rubric.description: Complete, polished, on time, and clearly meets or extends the stated requirements.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but incomplete, unclear, or not functional.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Record a 20-second screen capture showing smooth movement from one object to another and ending in Game View.
reflectionPrompt: Which navigation shortcut helped you the most today?

title: Starter Scene Transform Challenge
instructions: Build a simple starter scene using starter content objects and transform tools.
requiredSteps: Use starter content objects or simple shapes to build a small scene.
Move at least three objects with the W shortcut or move gizmo.
Rotate at least two objects with the E shortcut or rotate gizmo.
Scale at least one object with the R shortcut or scale gizmo.
Duplicate at least one object using Ctrl+D or Alt-drag.
Capture a screenshot of the completed simple scene and write a short reflection naming at least three tools or shortcuts used.
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of completed simple scene
Short reflection naming at least three tools or shortcuts used

rubric.description: Complete, polished, on time, and clearly meets or extends the stated requirements.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but incomplete, unclear, or not functional.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Use snapping intentionally to align objects and add one note explaining where snapping helped your scene.
reflectionPrompt: Name three tools or shortcuts you used, and explain which one helped your scene most.

title: First Scene Cleanup and Evidence Upgrade
skillFocus: Quiz feedback helps identify what needs review
Viewport navigation helps check the scene from useful angles
Transform tools fix placement, scale, and rotation issues
The Outliner helps select, organize, and rename visible objects
Screenshots document evidence for teacher review
instructions: Complete or review Quiz 01, then improve the simple scene you built in Lesson 04 with stronger organization, cleaner object placement, and clearer final evidence.
requiredSteps: Complete or review Quiz 01 for Lessons 01-04.
Open the simple scene built in Lesson 04.
Clean up object placement.
Fix floating or intersecting objects.
Improve the camera angle for the final screenshot.
Organize visible evidence so the scene is easier to understand.
Take a stronger final screenshot.
Write a short reflection naming at least three tools or shortcuts used.
evidenceRequired: Before screenshot, if available.
After screenshot.
Short reflection naming at least three tools or shortcuts.
Optional quiz completion screenshot or teacher checkoff.

rubric.description: Complete, polished, on time, and clearly meets or extends the stated requirements.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but incomplete, unclear, or not functional.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Create one portfolio-ready screenshot with a clear camera angle, readable object placement, and no distracting editor clutter.
reflectionPrompt: Name at least three Unreal tools or shortcuts you used, then explain how they improved the scene.

title: Post-Process Exposure Test
skillFocus: Post Process Volume applies camera and image effects
Metering Mode can be set to Manual for more control
Infinite Extent Unbound lets the volume affect the whole level
Bloom, Vignette, and Saturation change the image style
instructions: Duplicate a simple level or create a small test area, build a hut or dark-space exposure example, then use post-process settings to control exposure and one visual effect.
requiredSteps: Create or duplicate a level for post-process testing.
Build a simple hut or dark/bright test space.
Observe auto exposure behavior.
Add or select a post-process volume.
Set metering mode to manual.
Adjust exposure compensation.
Enable infinite extent unbound when appropriate.
Experiment with at least one effect such as bloom, vignette, or saturation.
evidenceRequired: Screenshot showing auto exposure/problem situation.
Screenshot showing manual exposure/post-process result.
Short reflection explaining what changed.

rubric.description: Complete, polished, on time, and clearly meets or extends the stated requirements.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but incomplete, unclear, or not functional.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Create two mood versions of the same view, such as neutral exposure and dramatic high-contrast exposure, and label the setting that changed.
reflectionPrompt: Explain which post-process setting changed the scene most and how your screenshots prove it.

title: First Custom Material
skillFocus: The Material Editor uses a node graph
Base Color controls the main visible color
Roughness controls how shiny or matte the surface feels
Metallic controls whether the surface behaves like metal
Apply and Save update the material for the level
instructions: Create a custom material, connect color and scalar values in the material graph, apply the material to an object, and submit evidence of both the graph and the result in the level.
requiredSteps: Create or open a material example level.
Create a new material using naming convention M_.
Open the material graph.
Add a color/vector value to base color.
Add scalar values for roughness and metallic.
Press Apply and Save after changes.
Apply the material to an object.
Optional: add a noise/texture sample to break up roughness or color variation.
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the material graph.
Screenshot of the material applied to an object.
Short reflection explaining base color, roughness, and metallic.

rubric.description: Complete, polished, on time, and clearly meets or extends the stated requirements.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but incomplete, unclear, or not functional.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Create a comparison board with three objects using the same base color but different roughness values.
reflectionPrompt: Explain what Base Color, Roughness, and Metallic each changed in your material.

title: Texture Map Material Build
skillFocus: Texture assets appear in the Content Drawer
Texture samples feed image data into the material graph
Base Color maps carry color information
Roughness and Normal maps carry non-color data
Texture Coordinate and Multiply nodes can adjust tiling or scale
instructions: Import or use provided texture maps, check basic texture settings, create a material using base color, roughness, and normal maps, then adjust tiling or scale using texture coordinates or a multiply node.
requiredSteps: Import or locate a base color texture.
Import or locate a roughness texture.
Import or locate a normal map.
Confirm sRGB is on for color textures.
Confirm sRGB is off for roughness/masks and normal maps.
Confirm normal map compression/settings if applicable.
Create a material using M_ naming convention.
Connect base color to Base Color.
Connect roughness to Roughness.
Connect normal map to Normal.
Use texture coordinate/multiply setup or equivalent to adjust tiling/scale.
Apply and save the material.
Apply it to a floor or object.
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of imported texture assets.
Screenshot of material graph connections.
Screenshot of material applied in the level.
Short reflection explaining what the normal map adds.

rubric.description: Complete, polished, on time, and clearly meets or extends the stated requirements.
Complete and meets the stated requirements.
Partially complete or missing required evidence.
Attempted but incomplete, unclear, or not functional.
Not submitted.

extensionChallenge: Create two versions of the same material with different tiling values and label which one feels more believable for the object scale.
reflectionPrompt: Explain what the normal map adds to the material and why the non-color map settings matter.

instructions: Set up the Q3 Unreal production project and define a clear goal for the castle environment.

title: Castle Environment Plan
instructions: Create a plan for a fantasy/castle environment using references, constraints, and a clear focal point.
requiredSteps: Collect teacher-approved visual references.
Identify the environment mood and main focal point.
Sketch or describe the basic layout.
List constraints or assets you expect to use.
evidenceRequired: Reference board or planning document
Simple layout sketch or written scene plan

title: Third-Person Walkthrough Setup

Confirm the playable character can move in the level.
Check that the environment scale feels appropriate.
Capture a screenshot showing the player character.
